title: Measurement of biogenic silica from plankton
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6mpil","text":"Here, we present a method for measuring biogenic silica from plankton. Biogenic silica is digested using a wet-alkaline method, in which 2 M sodium carbonate is used to hydrate and depolymerize amorphous silica, resulting in the production of monosilicic acid. The molybdate measurement technique is based on the method described by Shemesh et al. (1988) and follows the JGOFS protocols outlined by UNESCO (1994).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a3h6e","text":"To ensure the accuracy of the measurement, Celite S diatomaceous earth is used as a check standard for the recovery of biogenic silica. Our method yields a recovery rate of 85% to 90%.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"sg3p","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"eljl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"88mn2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"lgmr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"citation","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"authors":"Shemesh, Aldo; Mortlock, Richard A; Smith, R J; Froelich, Philip N","can_edit":1,"date":"1988-12-20T00:00:00Z","doi":"https://doi.org/10.1016/0304-4203(88)90113-2","guid":"4962E651341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","journal":"Marine Chemistry","protocol_id":null,"title":"Determination of Ge/Si in marine siliceous microfossils: separation, cleaning and dissolution of diatoms and radiolaria"}}}}
steps: Estimation: The low limit of detection is approximately 0.6 uM silicate in the molybdate method. For siliceous plankton, sample requires no less than 4 ug PON (particulate organic nitrogen) per filter when using a 50 mL volumetric flask, or 2 ug PON per filter when using a 25 mL volumetric flask. The sampling volume for biogenic silica samples is approximately 10% of the PON sample volume. For seawater samples, the sampling volume for biogenic silica samples should be determined based on the community composition.
Filter plankton sample in liquid media onto polycarbonate filters, using gentle vacuum pressure (130 mmHg) Filter forceps (blunt end, stainless steel, Millipore, XX6200006P)
Rinse filter funnel with filtered artificial seawater without macronutrients
Transfer filter with sample into 2 mL cryogenic vial
Filter blank media (without cells, same volume as plankton samples) through polycarbonate filter as blank
Flash freeze and store at -20 °C
2 M Na 2 CO 3 (18.69%) Need to be freshly prepared. The old reagent can yield high blank possibly by leaching silicate from plastic material.
Each sample requires 10 mL 2 M Na 2 CO 3
Tare a 1 L plastic erlenmeyer flask
Transfer Na 2 CO 3 into the flask
Top to 1000 g with MilliQ and shake until all salt is completely dissolved.
Turn on airforce oven to 85 °C

In the fume hood, transfer diatomaceous into a 5 mL plastic tube for weighing convenience (the original package is 1 kg). Diatomaceous is used as a check standard for the recovery of biogenic silica Diatomaceous is hygroscopic, it needs to be stored in the vacuum desiccator Diatomaceous: Upper respiratory irritant. May cause coughing or throat irritation. Breathing dust containing crystalline silica over a long period may cause lung damage. Celite S diatomaceous earth Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #06858 (CAS: 61790-53-2)

Weigh 100~200 ug diatomaceous into 50 mL falcon tube, in triplicate. Log the actual weight. Do not open the container until the static charge of diatomaceous powder has been neutralized by ionization blower. Less than 100 ug sample might introduce more error amongst the replicates in recovery.
Prepare one empty 50 mL falcon tube as the reagent blank for diatomaceous.
Add 10 mL 2 M Na 2 CO 3 to each tube, including: reagent blank for check standards check standards blank for samples samples
Loose the caps and place all tubes into the airforce oven overnight (for example, from 5 pm to 9 am).
Molybdate reagent stock solution Require 100 uL per sample
Ammonium molybdate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #09878-100G (CAS: 12054-85-2) Ammonium paramolybdate: [(NH4)6Mo7O24.4H2O] CAS: 12054-85-2
Add 1.6 g ammonium paramolybdate into a 125 mL plastic bottle and top to 100 g with MilliQ.
Store out of direct sunlight. Discard if white precipitation forms.
HCl stock solution Require 100 uL per sample
Metol-sulfite solution Require 100 uL per sample
Require: (1) 50 mL syringe (2) Syringe filter Syringe filter (0.2 um PES, VWR, 28145-501)
In a 100 to 250 mL plastic beaker, add 0.6 g sodium sulphite.
Add 1 g 4-(methyl amino)phenol hemisulfate.
Top to 50 g with MilliQ water.
Fill syringe with Metol-sulfite solution, filter through the syringe filter, collect filtrate into four 15 mL falcon tubes wrapped with foil, keep at room temperature.
Prepare fresh every month.
Oxalic acid solution Require 100 uL per sample.
Oxalic acid dihydrate VWR International (Avantor) Catalog #BDH4556-500G (CAS: 6153-56-6)
In a 125 mL plastic bottle, add 6 g oxalic acid and top to 100 g.
Let the solution stand at room temperature overnight.
Decant the solution from the crystals into a plastic bottle.
Keep at room temperature.
Sulphuric acid (30%)
Require 100 uL per sample
Mix 3 part concentrated sulphuric acid into 7 part of MilliQ Cool down to room temperature This can be prepared on Day 2 prior to molybdate reaction
Primary silica standard solution (~ 1 mM Si)
Sodium hexafluorosilicate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #250171 (CAS: 16893-85-9)
Transfer 1 g sodium fluorosilicate in a plastic vial
Keep the vial in a vacuum desiccator overnight to remove excess water (do not heat or fuse)
In a one litre plastic volumetric flask, dissolve ~ 0.1881 g (log the actual mass) of dry sodium fluorosilicate in MilliQ water and top to 1 L with MilliQ water.
It takes about 30 min to complete the dissolution. This cannot be rushed.
Store in a plastic bottle at room temperature.
Volume of 12 N HCl required: about 3.5 mL X N

Transfer resulted solution from falcon tube to 25 or 50 mL polypropylene volumetric flask. If the color of solution change back to orange, add more drops of HCl until the color turns to permanent pink again. Be careful while transferring the solution and ensure that the filter does not fall out of the tube, which spills the solution and causes sample loss.

Use MilliQ to rinse the tube three times and transfer all samples into the volumetric flask. If a 50 mL volumetric flask is used, rinse the falcon tube with 5 mL of MilliQ at a time. If a 25 mL volumetric flask is used, rinse the falcon tube with 1 mL of MilliQ at a time.
Use transfer pipet, top final volume to 25 or 50 mL with Milli-Q.
Shake and thoroughly mix the solution. Before mixing, check the cap to avoid leaking
Secondary standard solution (Freshly prepared prior to the assay) 50 uL primary stock solution 450 uL MilliQ
Molybdate working solution Require 200 uL per sample
1 part Molybdate stock reagent 1 part HCl stock reagent
Reducing solution Require 300 uL per sample
1 part Metol-sulfite solution 1 part oxalic acid solution 1 part sulphuric acid solution
Add 200 µL Molybdate reagent into each tube.
Add 300 µL reducing solution into each tube.
Vortex each tube and then shake at Room temperature for 03:00:00

In the fume hood, vortex each tube and then load 250 µL of the sample into one well of the microplate. Vortex again and load the same sample into another well of the microplate as replicate. 96-Well Microplates, Polystyrene, Clear, (Greiner Bio-One, 655101)

Setup the program %s %s AB1Shake duration00:00:052Shaking typeContinuous3Shaking forceHigh4Shaking speed [rpm]6005Wavelength [nm]8206Use transmittanceNo7Pathlength correctionNo8Measurement Time [ms]100 Varioskan LUX Multimode Microplate Reader (Thermo Fisher, VL0L00D0)
Collect all solution with paramethylaminophenol sulphate and sodium fluorosilicate into the waste container.
Subtract the average absorbance at 812 nm of the blank standard replicates from the absorbance at 812 nm of all other standard working solutions.
Subtract the average absorbance at 812 nm of the blank sample (i.e. blank filter) replicates from the absorbance at 812 nm of all other individual samples.
Prepare a standard curve by plotting the average blank-corrected 812 nm absorbance for each standard working solution versus its concentration in uM.
Use the standard curve to determine the silicate concentration of each unknown sample by using its blank-corrected 812 nm absorbance.

Si per sample = Si X V X (0.001) X DF Variable Unit Definition 1 Si per sample umol element Si in the sample collected 2 Si uM silicate concentration calculated from the standard curve 3 V mL volume of volumetric flask 4 DF From volumetric flask to the microtube, DF=10

% Diatomaceous recovery = 100 X Si X V X (0.001) X MW X DF /(M_ug X Purity X 0.01) Variable Unit Definition 1 % Diatomaceous recovery percentage recovery of diatomaceous 2 Si uM silicate concentration calculated from the standard curve 3 V mL volume of volumetric flask 4 MW ug/umol molecular mass of SiO2, i.e. 60.08 5 DF From volumetric flask to the microtube, DF=10 6 M ug actual mass of diatomaceous 7 Purity purity of SiO2 in Celite S diatomaceous earth (06858) is 90.2% The recovery should be around 85 to 90%.
Flash freeze and store at -20 °C
Transfer filter into 2 mL cryogenic vial
Transfer solution from volumetric flask to a clean and labelled Falcon tube.
Vortex each tube and then shake at Room temperature for 00:15:00 for the formation of silicomolybdic acid.

Standard working solutions (Freshly prepared prior to the assay) Standards Secondary (uL) MilliQ (uL) Conc. (uM) 1 S1 0 500 0 2 S2 5 495 1 3 S3 10 490 2 4 S4 20 480 4 5 S5 40 460 8 6 S6 60 440 12 7 S7 80 420 16 8 S8 100 400 20
Use graduated cylinder, measure 95 mL MilliQ and transfer into a 125 mL plastic bottle.
In the fume hood, add 5 mL 12 N HCl into the bottle, mix well.
4-(methylamino)phenol hemisulfate salt Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #320013 (CAS: 55-55-0) Sodium sulfite Fisher Scientific Catalog #S430-500 (CAS: 7757-83-7)
The addition of reagent must be operated in the fume hood. Acidified sodium fluorosilicate may contain some hydrofluoric acid.
Vortex
Dropwise add 3 mL 12 N HCl by using 1000 uL pipet. Do it slowly. Swirl the tube until reaction stops and then add the next drop. The most vigorous reaction is at about 3 mL 12 N HCl.
Sample dilution

....Measure pH of each sample (in the Falcon tube) Sample code (example) Sample code pH 1 Blank for check standards 2 Check standard 1 3 Check standard 2 4 Check standard 3 5 Blank for samples 6 Sample 1 7 Sample 2 8 ...
Weigh 186.9 g Na 2 CO 3 in a weighing dish. (CAS: 497-19-8, FW 105.99)
Aliquot the solution into four 250 mL plastic bottles.

Switch to a 100 uL pipette, add 100 uL at a time. Near the equivalence point, when the colour starts to change to pink more markedly but after mixing the orange colour returns, it is necessary to add HCl drop by drop . The first drop that causes a permanent colour change to pink determines the equivalence point. Stop adding HCl. We have found that the optimal pH for the reaction between silicate and molybdate to form silicomolybdic acid is 3 to 4. Too low or too high pH decreases recovery of biogenic silica. The acidified solution yields pH at 2 to 3. It is diluted to 10% in the molybdate assay, which gives pH at 3 to 4.
In the fume hood, add 30 µL Methyl orange into the tube. Methyl orange Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #1013230250
Work on one tube at a time, and leave other tubes in the oven.
Transfer 12 N HCl into a 50 mL Falcon tube in the fume hood.
Setup the layout.
Rinse microtubes and microplate with tap water, dispose in blue recycling bin.
Read the samples.
Export data sheet to excel.
Add MilliQ until the volume of solution in the falcon tube is 10 mL. The original volume of Na2CO3 is reduced due to evaporation of water during 20-h dissolution.

Fold the filter with two tweezers: (1) Fold in half along its diameter, creating a semicircular shape; (2) Fold once more in the same direction, resulting in a long strip; (3) Fold once more, halving its length, so that sample is secured.

Transfer sample to 50 mL falcon tube with clean filter forceps (rinsed by 95% ethanol and air-dried), dry at 85 °C in the airforce oven. Prior to transferring: (1) For filters folded into half-strip, unfold once to return to a strip. (2) For filters folded into quarter-circles, unfold once to return to a half-circle shape, then fold once along the dimension to form a strip. (2) For filters haphazardly into a compact mass, carefully unfold with two tweezers (avoiding losing biomass), fold once into a half-cricle shape, then fold once more along the dimension to form a strip Forced air oven (VWR, 89511-410) Falcon® Centrifuge Tubes (Polypropylene, Sterile, 50 mL, Corning®, 352070)
Vortex and then transfer 50 µL from the following solutions to labelled 2 mL microtubes. (1) blank for check standards, (2) check standards, (3) blank for samples, (4) samples
Add 450 µL MilliQ into each tube to obtain a 10% dilution.
